title: Configuring the auto-indentation feature
text: Configuring the auto-indentation feature Auto-indent is an automated feature in the Uniface Script Editor that automatically adjusts the indentation of Proc lines, ensuring consistent and readable formatting. This feature is enabled by default and can be disabled by setting autoindent=off in the usys.ini file. When pressing Enter at the end of a line, auto-indent will automatically add indentation to the new line. The new line will have the same indentation as the line above. If the line above starts a new scope, such as if , for , while , variables , etc., then the new line will have an additional tab or spaces. If the line above terminates a scope, such as endif , endfor , endwhile , endvariables , etc., then the line above will also be auto indented. Note: There are a few limitations with auto-indentation: The HTML language, JavaScript blocks, and blockdata blocks do not support auto-indentation. When using inline-if statements or inline-while statements, always make sure that brackets surround the conditional statements. To ensure that new lines are automatically indented, edit the Script Editor definition in the usys.ini file and ensure that autoindent is set as on as shown below: Explain this code ;usys.ini [widgets] ... SourceEdit=usrcedit(font=procboxfont;language=uniface;autoindent=on) Copy Autoindent— new lines will be automatically indented; default is on. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
